Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding 168 upvotes, 26 comments
  2. My Journey to a reliable and enjoyable locally hosted voice assistant (2025) 290 upvotes, 91 comments
  3. Lazycut: A simple terminal video trimmer using FFmpeg 136 upvotes, 43 comments
  4. MoD sources warn Palantir role at heart of government is threat to UK security 558 upvotes, 225 comments
  5. The “small web” is bigger than you might think 259 upvotes, 103 comments
  6. Meta’s renewed commitment to jemalloc 281 upvotes, 117 comments
  7. Why I love FreeBSD 309 upvotes, 139 comments
  8. Language Model Teams as Distrbuted Systems 57 upvotes, 19 comments
  9. Comparing Python Type Checkers: Typing Spec Conformance 84 upvotes, 33 comments
  10. Corruption erodes social trust more in democracies than in autocracies 615 upvotes, 315 comments
  11. Launch HN: Chamber (YC W26) – An AI Teammate for GPU Infrastructure 19 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. Home Assistant waters my plants 235 upvotes, 123 comments
  13. On The Need For Understanding 59 upvotes, 24 comments
  14. US commercial insurers pay 254% of Medicare for the same hospital procedures 51 upvotes, 21 comments
  15. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 178 upvotes, 95 comments
  16. Polymarket gamblers threaten to kill me over Iran missile story 1218 upvotes, 807 comments
  17. Show HN: Claude Code skills that build complete Godot games 114 upvotes, 63 comments
  18. Kona EV Hacking 111 upvotes, 63 comments
  19. Speed at the cost of quality: Study of use of Cursor AI in open source projects (2025) 81 upvotes, 44 comments
  20. Nvidia Launches Vera CPU, Purpose-Built for Agentic AI 91 upvotes, 59 comments
  21. Launch HN: Voygr (YC W26) – A better maps API for agents and AI apps 56 upvotes, 33 comments
  22. US Job Market Visualizer 375 upvotes, 299 comments
  23. Starlink Mini as a failover 155 upvotes, 143 comments
  24. Apideck CLI – An AI-agent interface with much lower context consumption than MCP 108 upvotes, 101 comments
  25. Show HN: Thermal Receipt Printers – Markdown and Web UI 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  26. Show HN: Oxyde – Pydantic-native async ORM with a Rust core 19 upvotes, 13 comments
  27. The bureaucracy blocking the chance at a cure 73 upvotes, 105 comments
  28. AirPods Max 2 162 upvotes, 311 comments
